blocks.body: Tenses help you say when something happens. Start with Present Simple and Present Continuous.

{"level":"A1–A2","intro":"Tenses help you show when something happens and whether it repeats or is happening now.","rules":[{"label":"Time meaning","color":"emerald","text":"Choose a tense to show the time or kind of action.","example":"every day, now, at the moment, this week"},{"label":"Present Simple","color":"blue","text":"Start with the tense for habits, facts, and repeated actions.","example":"I work every day. / She lives in Warsaw."},{"label":"Present Continuous","color":"amber","text":"Then move to actions happening now or around the present time.","example":"I am studying now. / They are working this week."}]}

{"title":"1. Starter subsections","intro":"Begin with two core tenses for A1–A2.","items":[{"label":"A1–A2","title":"Present Simple","subtitle":"Forms, negatives, questions, and adverbs of frequency."},{"label":"A1–A2","title":"Present Continuous","subtitle":"Forms, negatives, questions, and time expressions."}]}

{"title":"2. In short","items":["Tenses show when an action or state happens.","Present Simple gives you the base for habits, facts, and repeated actions.","Present Continuous adds actions happening now or around the present time.","Together, these two subsections cover the first starter block for A1–A2."]}
